title: Purpose of the Iwi Statistical Standard and Classification | Stats NZ
content:
## Purpose of the Iwi Statistical Standard and Classification
24 September 2018, 12:00pm
A revised version of the Iwi Statistical Standard and Classification was published in September 2017.
As a Treaty partner and the country’s national statistics office, Stats NZ has a role to provide information and statistics on and for iwi and iwi-related groups.
In September 2017 Stats NZ published the Iwi Statistical Standard and Classification of Iwi and Iwi-related Groups after completing the first comprehensive review of the standard and classification since it was developed in 1994. The review focused on ensuring the standard will continue to meet the current and future data needs of all users (including iwi and Māori) and producers of official statistics including Stats NZ.
A more inclusive classification of iwi and iwi-related groups has been developed so that we can continue to do this and ensure that more of the data being collected by government for and about Māori is accessible not only by government but by Māori and iwi organisations.
Changes made to the standard were guided by feedback from iwi, Māori, government, local government, and the public, as well as advice from an advisory group of Māori and government agency members. The main changes include:
- highlighting that the standard is for statistical purposes
- updating the definitions and guidelines for using the standard
- updating the classification (list), which included the addition of 32 groups.
It is important to highlight that including a group in the classification does not mean the group is recognised as an iwi in every context – such as in legislation, in other recognised iwi lists, or by other Māori authorities.
The standard helps us to provide data about and for Māori in meaningful ways, and not determine who is and who is not an iwi. The iwi standard, which includes the list of iwi and iwi-related groups, is very clear that it is designed for statistical purposes. Groups in the classification may or may not be considered iwi in other situations.

## For more information
See Aria for the Iwi Statistical Standard and Classification.
Alternatively, you can contact us at Stats NZ (04) 931 4600 or email classifications@stats.govt.nz
